marshmallow.name: MARSHMALLOW
marshmallow.title: Marshmallow
marshmallow.description: Marshmallow is a fun, forgiving beach break on Sri Lanka's south coast. It is popular with beginners, surf schools, and surfers looking for soft playful waves close to shore.
marshmallow.waves: Soft, rolling waves breaking over sand. Marshmallow has a mellow takeoff zone and forgiving conditions, making it ideal for learning, improving fundamentals, and enjoying relaxed surf sessions.
marshmallow.hazards: Generally one of the safer breaks in the area thanks to its sandy bottom. Main hazards are crowds during peak season, beginner traffic in the lineup, and occasional currents when larger swells are running.
marshmallow.best: Clean morning winds and small-to-medium south-west swells usually provide the best conditions. Marshmallow works across a wide tide range but is often most user-friendly around mid tide.

host.name: Follow The White Rabbit
host.description: Established in 2018 and renowned for its diverse menu, the restaurant offers freshly prepared, locally sourced dishes, with vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free options. Whether it’s brunch, tapas, or dinner, savor each bite as you overlook Ahangama’s stunning beach.

featuredPartners.title: Surf School : Salt n Surf Ahangama
featuredPartners.description: Surf Coach for Every Level, whitewash to pro, with video feedback!

featuredPartners.title: Surf Photography
featuredPartners.description: Water photography, beach shots, and drone clips for your best waves.

services.title: Scooter Rental
services.description: Daily scooter rentals close to the surf spot.

services.title: Surf Shop
services.description: Wax, fins, leashes, ding repair, and board rentals nearby.

services.title: Accommodation
services.description: Guesthouses and rooms within walking distance of the break.

mirissa-point.name: MIRISSA POINT
mirissa-point.title: Mirissa Point
mirissa-point.description: Mirissa Surf Point is a super fun and scenic right-hand reef break. The wave peels cleanly, making it easy to catch and ride. On a good swell, the wave opens up with nice speed, occasionally offering clean, hollow little barrels. It is a fantastic spot to get long rides and enjoy a great tropical session, making it best suited for intermediate to advanced surfers.

areas.title: Primary Entrance & Paddle-Out Route
areas.description: This is the safest and easiest entrance to the surf point. Before paddling out, pick out a landmark on the shore—a distinct tree works perfectly. Having a clear landmark will help you navigate back to your exit point when you come in. Hazard: always be aware of Rock 8, which sits at surface level and can be hard to see depending on the tide. Stay 2 to 4 feet to the right side of Rock 8 and do not drift too far past it, as beyond this point the reef becomes very shallow and heavy. Seasonal changes also affect this entrance: sometimes it is covered in sand, while other times rocks are exposed. If walking over rocks, watch for sea urchins.

areas.title: Alternative Beach Entrance
areas.description: Alternative entry point that works when the reef is covered by sand. However, this sand cover is rare, leaving behind sharp and shallow reef. Unless you are certain of safe sand coverage, this entrance is not recommended.

areas.title: Outer Paddle-Out Channel (Long Route)
areas.description: A longer but safer paddle-out route that takes you from the beach, around the main rock formation, and into the lineup. Recommended for beginners or surfers with limited board control. Always maintain distance from rocks, especially during high swell conditions.

areas.title: Impact Zone Rock
areas.description: A heavily exposed rock covered in sea urchins. Never walk or climb on it. If a wave pushes you toward this area, kick out early or move into deeper water before reaching the rock.

areas.title: Rip Current / Deep Water Passage
areas.description: Strong natural current formed by water flowing from multiple areas. On larger days, paddling straight back out from inside sections can be very difficult. Use this current to your advantage by moving into it and allowing it to carry you back toward the main lineup.

areas.title: General Impact Zone (Shallow Reef)
areas.description: A wide shallow reef area where falling often results in foot contact with the bottom. Never dive headfirst here. If you must walk on the reef, move carefully and be aware of sea urchins.

areas.title: Main Peak / Lineup
areas.description: Primary take-off zone where waves break. This area can get crowded, so maintain awareness, respect other surfers, and avoid dropping in on locals.

areas.title: Surface Rock
areas.description: A hazardous rock sitting at water level. Avoid it completely when entering or exiting the surf zone, especially when returning to shore.

safety: Always check swell, tide, and reef exposure before entering the water.
Use a clear shoreline landmark to orient your entry and exit point.
Avoid Rock 8 and shallow reef zones at all times.
Do not dive headfirst in shallow reef areas.
Respect local surfers and avoid drop-ins at the main peak.
Sea urchins are common in exposed reef sections—move carefully if walking.

host.name: A++ Restaurant
host.description: Beachfront restaurant in Mirissa, Sri Lanka. Located right in front of the main surf point, it offers an incredible mix of Sri Lankan, Mexican, and European dishes, alongside excellent coffee and cocktails, all with stunning ocean views.

featuredPartners.title: Eli's Stroopwafels & Coffee
featuredPartners.description: Freshly made Sroopwafels & Coffee. Taste a bit of The Netherlands in Mirissa!

services.title: Board Rental
services.description: Shortboards, funboards, and longboards available nearby.

temple-point.name: TEMPLE POINT
temple-point.title: Temple Point
temple-point.description: Temple Point is a beautiful surf zone with clear water, tropical scenery, and changing conditions depending on swell and tide. On the right day, this spot can produce beautiful A-frame peaks, and occasionally even some fun barrels.

areas.title: Main Entrance (Recommended)
areas.description: This is the easiest way to access the surf point. You can walk down through the mountain path from Odin's Restaurant. At the bottom, you will find a couple of rocks that you need to cross carefully. They can be very slippery, especially when wet. When exiting the surf point, it is usually better to use Route 2 and head all the way to the beach. During larger surf, it can be difficult to exit safely from the same place you entered at Point 1.

areas.title: Alternative Entrance
areas.description: Another way to reach the surf point is from Sunset Bar Beach. You can enter the water there and swim across. While passing this area, watch out for two rocks that sit near the surface. At times, you may experience a current drifting toward the Parrot Rock side. It is usually not dangerous, but do not allow it to carry you too far. Try to stay in deeper water and make your way toward Area 4.

areas.title: Rocky Entrance
areas.description: This is another possible entrance to the surf point. However, there are many rocks in this area, so proceed carefully and always be aware of your surroundings.

areas.title: Main Lineup
areas.description: This is the main surfing area. You can catch both left and right waves from this position, directly in front of the rocks. This is generally the best place to wait for waves.

areas.title: Mountain Reef (Danger Zone)
areas.description: Always stay clear of this area. The Mountain Reef is shallow and can be dangerous, especially during larger swells. If you find yourself drifting onto the reef, paddling straight out may be difficult. Instead, move left or right with the current and work your way back toward Area 4 before heading out.

safety: Always check the conditions before entering the water. Currents, wave size, and reef exposure can change throughout the day.

Make sure your leash is in good condition before surfing. If your leash breaks, there is a high chance you will not recover your board without damage. In some situations, you may also need assistance from other surfers to get back safely.

If you lose your board, do not panic. If it is close enough, try to recover it safely. If it is too far away, forget the board and focus on your own safety. Exit the surf point using Route 1, 2, or 3, follow the current, and remain calm.
This surf break is generally not beginner friendly, especially if you are surfing alone. It is best suited for intermediate to advanced surfers.

host.name: ODIN'S
host.description: Odin near Coconut Tree Hill is pure slow mood. Coconut trees moving to their own rhythm, waves on loop, sunlight doing magic on the table. Peak Sri Lanka.

services.title: Local Food
services.description: Rice and curry, fresh juice, and post-surf meals nearby.

services.title: Transport
services.description: Tuk-tuk and scooter options for moving between surf spots.

weligama.name: WELIGAMA
weligama.title: Weligama
weligama.description: Weligama Bay is Sri Lanka's premier destination for beginner surfers. The sweeping 2-kilometer crescent beach provides mellow, rolling, sandy-bottom waves that are perfect for learning.

host.name: Samaru Beach Hotel
host.description: Samaru Beach House is a beach front guest house on the south coast of Sri Lanka, Weligama beach. This is a perfect and wonderful place for a relaxing holiday on beach in tranquil surroundings. Samaru Beach House stands for dedicated family hospitality at International standards with modern, clean and spacious rooms, clean and all immaculate to ensure an amazing time right on wonderful Weligama Beach.

services.title: Local Food
services.description: Rice and curry, fresh juice, and post-surf meals nearby.

services.title: Transport
services.description: Tuk-tuk and scooter options for moving between surf spots.

gas-station.name: GAS STATION - COMING SOON
gas-station.title: Gas Station
gas-station.description: Gas Station is a fun, consistent reef break right in front of the roadside fuel station. It offers playful walls on smaller days and faster, more punchy sections when the swell picks up. Best surfed on clean morning conditions with offshore winds, it’s a lively mix of good waves and Sri Lankan roadside chaos. Best for intermediate/advanced surfers

host.name: The Gas Point
host.description: Nestled in Ahangama, The Gas Point is a cozy spot to enjoy fresh seafood while soaking in breathtaking views of surfers and stilt fishermen. With a warm atmosphere and attentive staff, it's a great choice for those seeking a laid-back dining experience. Ideal for seafood lovers and anyone looking to unwind by the sea.

services.title: Local Food
services.description: Rice and curry, fresh juice, and post-surf meals nearby.

services.title: Transport
services.description: Tuk-tuk and scooter options for moving between surf spots.
